reasoning: Jamaican aged 8 yr, pot + column blend, ex-bourbon barrels, 43% ABV. Tasting: banana, coconut, toffee. Funk moderate (Appleton is balanced — pot-still ester present but not Wray-style aggressive). Bourbon-barrel vanilla.

Brazilian fresh-cane cachaça aged in amburana wood — native Brazilian hardwood giving 'cinnamon, clove, vanilla, tonka.' Producer notes describe it as effectively its own category. Vanilla driven by amburana, grassy from the cane-juice base (somewhat masked by aging).

Artisanal Brazilian cachaça, copper-alembic single-distilled from cane juice, no aging mentioned. 'Smooth and sweet with natural sugarcane aromas.' Pure cane-juice grassy character, low everything else.

Triple-distilled cinnamon rum with organic cinnamon, vanilla bean, sweet table molasses. Spiced-rum style — added molasses pushes molasses + sweet up; explicit vanilla bean → vanilla 2. Cinnamon character isn't captured by these axes (no spice axis for rum).

AOC Martinique agricole, 10 yr in virgin/re-charred oak, 44% ABV, 97pts Wine Enthusiast. The long aging adds significant oak/vanilla and mellows the grassy character somewhat — still clearly agricole but less raw than blanc agricole.

ba_name: Cruzan Estate Diamond Black Strap Rum
reasoning: Black strap molasses rum, 1-2 yr ex-bourbon, column-distilled since 1760. 'Rich molasses and ginger snap.' Defining the molasses=3 register. Modest oak/vanilla; not funky.

Reverse-engineered Mai Tai rum: 8 yr blend of Jamaican pot still (Worthy Park, Hampden — high ester) + Martinique Grande Arome agricole. High funk from the Jamaican component, some grassy from the agricole side.

ba_name: Doctor Bird Private Barrel Cask Strength Madeira Finish Rum (B&B Exclusive)
reasoning: B&B exclusive: Worthy Park unaged high-ester + aged medium-ester pot still rums, Madeira-cask finished, 59.5% ABV cask strength. Tasting: tropical fruit + Jamaican funk + Madeira-driven dried fruit/caramel/nuts. Max funk; Madeira adds sweet+vanilla.

Guyanese Demerara, min 15 yr. Blend from EHP Wooden Coffey, Metal Coffey, Port Mourant Double Wooden Pot Still, Versailles Single Wooden Pot Still. Nose: dark coffee, candied orange, almonds, dark chocolate, pepper, vanilla. Notoriously heavily added-sugar (sweet=3); long aging + dark Demerara character → max oak + vanilla.

Hawaiian fresh-cane-juice agricole-style. Producer notes: 'Grassy, vegetal, funky character distinct from Jamaican or Guyanese rums. Fills the agricole style gap.' Slight funk from fermentation but not Jamaican-style; unaged/lightly aged so low oak/vanilla.

Thai column-still single-cask, 11 yr, 100% molasses, 59.5% ABV cask strength. <190 bottles. Limited info on tasting notes; scored from build: column-still = less funk, long aging = high oak/vanilla, molasses base = moderate molasses register.

Haitian clairin (artisanal cane juice spirit) at 50% ABV. Field blend of indigenous cane clones, wild-yeast fermented 5-7 days. Wild fermentation adds funky/rustic character distinct from agricole; pure cane juice = high grassy, no molasses. Unaged so no oak/vanilla.

Traditional Jamaican pot still, Navy Strength (57%), Wedderburn + Plummer distillates (the two highest-ester categories in the Jamaican classification system). No added sugar, no chill filtration. The benchmark for funk=3 + sweet=0. Heavy pot-still register pushes molasses up.
